channel.title: Education News Today | The Hindu
channel.description: Today’s Education News: Get the latest news on higher education, board exams, results, admit cards, entrance exams, and admission news from The Hindu

item.title: India at the epicentre of global higher education: A call to action
NEET-UG plan: Transport of question papers under police escort, monitoring of coaching centres
item.description: Ministry of Education meets with officials to ensure NEET-UG exam integrity, implementing strict security measures for fair conduct

item.title: Surprise inspection finds none of the Class 10 students at Uttar Pradesh madrassa were able to write names in English
item.description: Surprise inspection reveals poor education quality at madrassa in Bahraich district, with class 10 students unable to write their names or the name of the madrassa in English

item.title: Know your English | What does ‘cock-a-hoop’ mean?
Hyderabad teens reach out to government school students; conduct workshops on creativity, communication skills
item.description: For Ishaan and Neha, class XI students of Chirec Public School, this journey of skilling the government school student started after the pandemic

item.title: The importance of encouraging students to read
item.description: It is essential for educators to actively nurture and promote reading so that students can reap the benefits

item.title: To get on the right career track
item.description: Students today have unparalleled access to resources for informed career decisions. Making full use of these lays the ground for long-term success

item.title: Career in Occupational Therapy
item.description: The scope of Occupational Therapy is expanding in India due to increased awareness of rehabilitation and wellness services

item.title: Future of CA profession is bright, given the growing demand for the professionals, say experts
item.description: Propelled by India’s projected economic expansion to $30 trillion within the next two decades, the requirement for skilled CAs is expected to surge dramatically, say industry leaders

item.title: New NCERT Class 7 Social Science textbook rolls three books of History, Geography, Social and Political life into one
item.description: Officials said Exploring Society - India and Beyond (Part 1) is meant for the first six months of Class 7; Part 2 likely to be released in upcoming months; Part 1’s history chapters end with Gupta era

item.title: Wildlife artist and illustrator Richa Kedia on following her passion
item.description: Nature teaches you to slow down, watch closely, and learn constantly.

item.title: A to-do list for the incoming UGC chairman
item.description: Appointment of a new Chairman for UGC is in the pipeline, sooner or later. We look at what would be on his plate.

item.title: Broaden your perspective
item.description: Uncertain about your career options? Low on self-confidence? This column may help

item.title: Even as overall numbers fall, Indian undergraduate student interest in U.S. holds firm
item.description: Overall Indian student admissions to the U.S. declined by 30% in 2025, while undergraduate numbers saw a much smaller decrease. Rising costs and visa challenges had a greater impact on postgraduate enrollments.

item.title: Scholarships: April 26, 2025
item.description: Information on financial aid for students

item.title: Why we need to teach students to design their thinking
item.description: Design Thinking’s true power lies not in its methodologies but in its potential to fundamentally reshape how students think and approach problems

item.title: Why we need the Humanities
item.description: Without ethics, aesthetics and hermeneutics that constitute the soul of the Humanities, the world will not be an ideal place for human beings

item.title: In 2024-25, IIT Madras filed 417 patents, doubling licencing revenue
item.description: The institute has filed 39 designs, 6 copyrights, and one trademark

item.title: The bitter truth about job losses caused by AI: Will reskilling help?
item.description: The Hindu webinar explores job losses due to AI, emphasizing reskilling and proactive discussions for a balanced approach.

item.title: Conclave on National Education Policy begins at NIT-C
Chennai school uses microgreens to make a portrait of A.P.J. Abdul Kalam
item.description: To commemorate the 10th anniversary of the institution, teachers and students of the Union Christian Public School came together to make a portrait of former Indian President A.P.J. Abdul Kalam with microgreens

item.title: Mysuru’s Ranga Rao Memorial School for Differently Abled invites applications from visually-challenged students
item.description: Ranga Rao Memorial School for Differently Abled has invited applications for admissions in the ensuing academic year

item.title: 93% of OIL-backed students clear JEE Mains
item.description: The petroleum major spends ₹3 lakh annually per engineering and medical aspirant across five Super 30 centres for students from underprivileged families

item.title: Karnataka CET: Document verification for candidates of special categories from May 5
item.description: Around 10,000 candidates have applied under special categories and various clauses

item.title: U.P. Board declares results for classes 10, 12
item.description: The overall pass percentage for class 10 students stood at 90.11%, while for class 12, it was 81.15%

item.title: Tirthahalli student Deeksha scores 600/600 in II PU after revaluation, tops science stream in Karnataka
item.description: Deeksha had scored 599/600 when the results were announced on April 8

item.title: ‘Bengaluru is a city thriving with innovation, strong academic institutions, and a spirit of progress’
item.description: The University of Wolverhampton has launched the Wolverhampton-Bengaluru Centre for Research and Innovation at Bangalore City University, marking a major step in international academic collaboration to drive research, innovation, and opportunities for students and faculty

item.title: PGETA score to determine MArch admissions in Karnataka
item.description: KEA will no longer conduct entrance exam for M.Arch admission

item.title: Karnataka to set up i-Code Labs to ensure computer education for students of government school
item.description: Initiative to bring students of government schools on par with their counterparts in private schools

item.title: News from the world of education: April 23, 2025
item.description: Information on admissions, scholarships, new courses, research studies and other events at schools and colleges

item.title: Union Health Ministry brings in new competency-based curricula for 10 allied and healthcare professions
item.description: India is skilling its healthcare professionals not just for India, but for the international market, says the Ministry; curricula of professions such as Physiotherapy, Optometry and Dialysis Therapy to see revision

item.title: Foundation lies in the books, go back to the basics: UPSC rank 8 holder Raj Krishna Jha
item.description: Raj Krishna Jha’s journey from a small village to securing rank 8 in UPSC with hard work and determination.

item.title: World Book Day 2025: 5 unmissable reads that cross borders and stay with you
Face-based Aadhaar authentication system for faculty attendance in medical colleges from May 1
item.description: New system expected to eliminate ghost faculty

item.title: U.S. federal court gives relief to Indian students whose visas were revoked
item.description: Georgia federal judge issues temporary restraining order restoring SEVIS records of 133 students terminated by ICE

item.title: CLAT UG-2025: Delhi High Court asks National Law Universities consortium to revise marksheets, republish result
item.description: The judgement came on a batch of petitions pointing out certain errors in the common law admission test (CLAT) UG-2025 questionnaire.

item.title: T.N. universities’ V-Cs in a dilemma over Governor’s invitation to higher education conference
item.description: The recent Supreme Court ruling has empowered the State government to appoint V-Cs but does not prevent the Governor from acting as the Chancellor, an expert points out

item.title: Pune, Kolkata film institutes are now deemed to be universities
item.description: The official status allows both institutions to launch Ph.D. programmes, research and innovative academic methods.

item.title: Wolverhampton-Bangalore Research and Innovation Centre inaugurated in Bengaluru
item.description: Prof. Suresh Renukappa, Director of the Centre, stated that the centre would focus on climate resilience, healthcare, smart cities and villages, energy security, waste-to-wealth initiatives, pollution control, and the Brand Bengaluru initiative

item.title: Into the maze
item.description: Taran and his family explore the Naida Caves of Diu with their strange rock formations, maze-like tunnels and more.

item.title: U.S. Vice-President J.D. Vance in India: His higher education story from Hillbilly Elegy explains Trump politics, MAGA
Finding our place: 5 must-reads that celebrate human-nature connections on World Earth Day
item.description: Explore the intricate bond between people and the planet with five remarkable reads from the British Council this Earth Day.

item.title: UPSC declares final result of 2024 civil services examination, three of top five are women
item.description: 1,009 candidates recommended for IAS, IFS, IPS, and Central Services; top 25 candidates took a range of optional subjects

item.title: U.S. visas issued to Indian students decline by 30% in Trump’s first month of presidency
item.description: The decline in the number of visas issued to Indian students was the highest among the top source countries by a significant margin

item.title: Mangaluru students excel in JEE Mains examination
With 100% in Maths, Kashmir’s JEE Mains topper Toiyeb Ashiq seeks to ace Advanced
item.description: Toiyeb Ashiq’s record-breaking brilliance in JEE Main 2025 symbolizes hope and possibility for Kashmir’s youth.

item.title: Student or teacher focus? The alternative pedagogy of the Upanishad
item.description: Investigating the concept of education in the age of edtech and edbiz, exploring historical roots and pedagogical traditions

item.title: Arts and science colleges in Tamil Nadu enter the arena of entrepreneurism
item.description: With the spread of start-up culture, colleges are improving their incubation cells and forging partnerships with the key players in the entrepreneurial ecosystem. As a result, more students pursuing arts and science courses are venturing into entrepreneurship

item.title: Three students from Coimbatore on how they won Monash University’s Change It Challenge
item.description: How the students designed a solution to address methane emissions from livestock farms

item.title: How Class 12 students aiming to study in the U.S. can navigate the year
item.description: A guide to make it through the year with minimum stress and maximum engagement

item.title: 525 Telangana Social Welfare Educational Institutions students qualify for JEE Advanced 2025
item.description: V.S. Alagu Varshini, Secretary of TGSWREIS, said that special coaching programmes were introduced to ensure Gurukula students receive JEE training on a par with top-tier private coaching centres

item.title: How to prepare for the CUET
item.description: A strategic plan, consistent effort, and a disciplined approach will help students meet the challenge of this entrance exam

item.title: Politics still rules Kottayam CMS college campus, although a little less now
item.description: From SFI chairman Alodin to KSU activist Jubin and neutral Bhavik, CMS College students reflect a changing tide in student political engagement.

item.title: Explained: How move to include Hindi from Class 1 has injected language politics into Maharashtra
item.description: Maharashtra mandates Hindi as third language in schools, sparking debate over language imposition and educational benefits.

item.title: Careers in wealth management
item.description: With rising affluence, increased disposable incomes, and a growing awareness of financial planning, this sector is experiencing a boom

item.title: Madras University syndicate seeks probe into ‘irregularities’ in purchase made by research centre
item.description: The government-appointed enquiry committee has not done its work properly, they allege

item.title: Don’t limit yourself
item.description: Uncertain about your career options? Low on self-confidence? This column may help

item.title: A free-wheeling chat with Prof. Don Norman on design and education
item.description: “Design is a way of thinking that can be applied to any problem,” says the American researcher and author emphasising that education has to move beyond conventional frameworks

item.title: Scholarships: April 19, 2025
item.description: Information on financial aid for students

item.title: Importance of using behavioural science to build better classrooms
item.description: How insights from behavioural science can be used to enhance learning outcomes and empower students with cognitive and emotional skills

item.title: Kushagra Gupta from Karnataka achieves 100 percentile score in JEE-2025 (main) Session-2
item.description: National Testing Agency (NTA) announced the result of JEE (Main)-2025, session-2 of Paper-1 for professional courses like BE/B.Tech in premier technical institutions like Indian Institute Technologies (IITs) on April 18

item.title: MEA raises issue of Indian students with U.S., after survey found 50% of students facing notices are Indians
item.description: Controversy brews ahead of U.S. V-P Vance’s visit; MEA tight-lipped about whether the student visa termination issue will be raised

item.title: Doctors seek roll-back of the double shift entrance exam model for NEET PG 2025
item.description: There is lack of transparency and allege disparity between shifts, say doctors

item.title: Karnataka CET 2025: Key answers published
item.description: The key answers for Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics and Biology have been published

item.title: NTA declares JEE (Mains) results, 24 candidates get 100 percentile score in exam
item.description: Based on the results of JEE (Main) Paper 1 and Paper 2, the candidates will be shortlisted to appear for JEE (Advanced), which is a one-stop exam for admission to the 23 premier Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs).

item.title: NCERT insists all new school textbooks were ‘named thoughtfully’
item.description: The response from National Council of Education Research and Training comes after a controversy erupted over NCERT using Hindi titles for English language textbooks

item.title: The bitter truth about job losses caused by AI
item.description: Join The Hindu’s live webinar to learn what you can do to avoid being swept away by the AI wave?

item.title: How AI is reshaping jobs and skills in Insurance and Retail?
item.description: How AI is reshaping insurance and retail careers, emphasizing the importance of domain knowledge and data accuracy.

item.title: Maharashtra Deputy CM Ajit Pawar slams opposition to Hindi in schools, says Marathi will still remain top priority
item.description: Deputy CM Ajit Pawar emphasises Marathi language importance, says opposition to Hindi imposition raking up “unnecessary dispute”

item.title: PG programme launched at IIM-K
Poetry, purpose, and passion: What Dead Poets Society reminds us about education
IIT Madras launches PG diploma in emerging technologies
item.description: Candidates who do well in the programme may upgrade to a web-enabled M.Tech degree at the institute

item.title: Centre asks coaching centres to stop misleading students and adhere to guidelines
item.description: CCPA warns coaching centres against misleading ads ahead of IIT-JEE results, enforces strict guidelines and penalties

item.title: Hindi now a mandatory third language in Maharashtra for classes 1-5 as it rolls out NEP 2020
item.description: The syllabus of the State Board will be based on the National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT), having Maharashtra’s local context in subjects like History, Geography, and languages

item.title: NEP 2020 goals: Why AI, computational thinking, and design thinking must be core to UG curriculum in Science, Arts, Law, Commerce .
item.description: Discover how Indian universities can stay ahead by integrating AI literacy, computational thinking, and design thinking into every undergraduate program — aligned with NEP 2020 goals.

item.title: TiE to host workshop on storytelling in Mysuru
item.description: The workshop will focus on ‘helping entrepreneurs and business professionals master the art of storytelling to drive impact, inspire action, and build lasting connections’

item.title: IIT-Bombay graduate launches free website to make ancient Sanskrit literature accessible to all
item.description: With the launch of SanskritSahitya.org, Antariksh Bothale, a software engineer and an IIT Bombay graduate is making ancient Sanskrit texts accessible and appealing to the new generation using modern computational tools.

item.title: News from the world of education: April 15, 2025
item.description: Information on admissions, scholarships, new courses, research studies and other events at schools and colleges

item.title: IIT, NIT placement data show job market stagnation: Congress
item.description: If one-fifth of the engineers at IITs are unable to find jobs, it raises concern over how the larger population of educated youth in this country is able to secure employment, says Jairam Ramesh

item.title: Too experienced to be hired? The harsh reality facing mid-career job seekers
item.description: Navigating mid-career job loss, ageism, and overqualification with practical advice and real stories to break the cycle effectively.

item.title: Doctoral distress: What’s broken in India’s PhD system and how to fix it
item.description: PhD programs in India face challenges with high drop-out rates, mental health issues, and lack of career guidance.

item.title: Fee hike issue: Delhi CM says notices issued to schools after complaints, warns of strict action
item.description: Addressing a 'Jan Samvad' (public meeting), the Chief Minister said she received a complaint about a private school in Model Town allegedly increasing fees and expelling students.

item.title: What is literacy rate?
item.description: When we hear the word literacy, we often imagine it means knowing a lot of things. But did you know that the simplest level of literacy starts with the ability to read and write basic sentences?

item.title: Chanakya University to host ‘Chanakya Samvad’ on global geo-politics
item.description: The keynote address will be delivered by Prof. Cord Jakobeit, Dean and Professor of Political Science at the University of Hamburg, Germany, who will present a European perspective on the shifting global order

item.title: Scoping NEP 2020: How English is integral to multilingual education
item.description: Unlock cognitive benefits of multilingualism in India, balancing English proficiency with preserving native languages for global academic success.

item.title: AITM Bhatkal hosts national-level 24-hour hackathon
item.description: Waseem Ahmed, an alumnus of AITM and the founder director of Wynteam Global, was the chief guest

item.title: Know your English | What does ‘listless’ mean?
Around 20% posts lying vacant across departments in Kashmir University, reveals RTI reply
item.description: The application filed by Jammu-based RTI activist Raman Kumar Sharma in February 2025 revealed that majority of the vacancies are at the faculty level

item.title: The importance of postgraduate courses for a career in research
item.description: A look at the various aspects that go into making a postgraduate course useful for students

item.title: Basics of a career in genomics research
item.description: With applications that go beyond healthcare, genomics research offers an array of opportunities from research and development to entrepreneurship and policy-making

item.title: Why working with nanomaterials is exciting
item.description: Gadhadar Reddy, Founder and CEO of NoPo Nanotechnologies, on his domain.

item.title: As CA aspirants dwindle, here’s why ICAI is conducting three CA final exams a year
item.description: ICAI’s move to conduct CA Final exams thrice a year from 2025 offers students more flexibility, while supporting India’s goal of producing 30 lakh CAs by 2047.

item.title: Tips to help aspirants prepare for NEET
item.description: How do you ensure that you retain all that you have learnt? Here are some strategies that might help

item.title: Build your skills
item.description: Uncertain about your career options? Low on self-confidence? This column may help

item.title: Scholarships: April 12, 2025
item.description: Information on financial aid for students

item.title: Importance of beginning gender-sensitive education early
item.description: Strategies for educational institutions to put gender sensitivity at the centre of classroom environments, teaching approaches, and pedagogy

item.title: Adult learning and the hierarchy of needs
item.description: Understanding their own needs and development as adult learners can help teachers relate better to their students

item.title: A.P. Intermediate exam results: Pass percentage of 83% among final-year students, 70% for first years
item.description: A total of 10,17,102 Intermediate students had appeared for the public examination

item.title: Giving shape to the university of the future
item.description: Creating a higher education structure in India that implements the vision of the National Education Policy 2020 will need to be a well-thought-out plan

item.title: School exam success rate: Concern over dip in Assam, jump in Meghalaya
item.description: Meghalaya’s high pass rate is criticised for potentially compromising education quality, while Assam’s drop in pass rate is seen as a failure of the government
